title: Mini-Grid Rural Electrification Viability In Sub-Saharan Africa
summary: Build a verified workflow for identifying where solar-hybrid mini-grids are technically and economically viable for rural electrification, linking settlement data, solar resource, demand estimates, and cost data at sub-national scale.
why_it_matters: Over 600 million people in Sub-Saharan Africa lack electricity access. Grid extension to remote rural areas is often uneconomic. Solar-hybrid mini-grids are the most viable solution for many communities, but site selection requires combining settlement location, solar resource, demand estimation, and cost modeling — a stacking of uncertainties that can produce dangerously wrong investment decisions.

success_metrics.target: Every viability factor links to dated evidence with methodology documented.
Viability maps include explicit uncertainty from each input layer.
No mini-grid investment recommendation merged without review and replication.
success_metrics.validation_method: Check task status and review records.

review_policy.merge_gate: Schema validation, domain review, safety review, replication.
